question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/contract/ContractResult.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Field: bloom (byte[])] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction that produces a bloom filter of exactly 256 bytes where all bytes are zero except one, causing setBloom() to store the near-empty bloom as non-empty, while a truly empty bloom is normalized away, creating an asymmetry in exported bloom data that breaks log filter queries?
